message:
Replace stub connector macro with explicit implementations for Amp, Claude Code, OpenCode

Convert three stub connectors from macro-generated types to explicit
struct/impl blocks, establishing consistent implementation pattern while
deferring real parsing logic to future commits.

Explicit stub structure (identical across all three):
```rust
pub struct <Name>Connector;

impl Default for <Name>Connector {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self::new()
    }
}

impl <Name>Connector {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self
    }
}

impl Connector for <Name>Connector {
    fn detect(&self) -> DetectionResult {
        DetectionResult::not_found()
    }

    fn scan(&self, _ctx: &ScanContext) -> Result<Vec<NormalizedConversation>> {
        Ok(Vec::new())
    }
}
```

Why explicit stubs instead of real implementations now:

AmpConnector (src/connectors/amp.rs):
Per PLAN § 2.4 and § 7.5, Amp stores conversation history primarily on
Sourcegraph servers with only partial local cache in VS Code globalStorage
and ~/.local/share/amp (Linux) or %APPDATA%\amp (Windows). Format is
underdocumented and exploratory scanning required. Stub until:
1. Real Amp users provide sample cache files for reverse engineering
2. Amp documentation clarifies local storage schema (currently sparse)
3. Codex/Cline/Gemini connectors validated end-to-end (prove framework
   works before tackling uncertain format)
Roadmap: v2+ (PLAN § 14.9 "remaining connectors")

ClaudeCodeConnector (src/connectors/claude_code.rs):
Per PLAN § 2.2 and § 7.3, Claude Code stores JSONL logs in ~/.claude/
projects/**/*.jsonl with additional per-repo .claude/.claude.json files.
Format similar to Codex (JSONL event stream) but session grouping
heuristics differ. Stub until:
1. Codex connector proves JSONL parsing patterns (reuse lessons learned)
2. Claude Code logs acquired from real users for schema validation
   (less community documentation than Codex/Cline)
3. Indexer orchestration tested with multiple connectors (ensure no
   Codex-specific assumptions in pipeline)
Roadmap: v1 (PLAN § 14.9 "remaining connectors" after Codex/Cline/Gemini)

OpenCodeConnector (src/connectors/opencode.rs):
Per PLAN § 2.6 and § 7.7, OpenCode stores conversations in SQLite
databases within project-local .opencode/ directories or global ~/.config/
opencode/ database. Requires rusqlite schema introspection (PRAGMA
table_info) to handle version differences, then straightforward SELECT
queries. Stub until:
1. OpenCode installations available for testing (less popular than
   Codex/Cline/Gemini in current user base)
2. SQLite connector pattern established (different from JSON/JSONL
   parsing, want to avoid premature abstraction)
3. Storage layer SQLite code stabilized (avoid conflicts with app's
   own rusqlite usage)
Roadmap: v1 (PLAN § 14.9 "remaining connectors")

Default trait implementation:
All three include Default::default() -> Self::new() for consistency with
Rust conventions (allows T::default() in generic contexts) even though
current usage is explicit new() calls. Future config-driven connector
instantiation may use Default (e.g., let connector = C::default() where
C: Connector + Default).

Clippy compliance:
new() returns Self (not AmpConnector) to avoid type annotation
redundancy. Detector and scan implementations use _ prefix on ctx
parameter (not bare ctx) to acknowledge unused argument and suppress
unused_variables lint in stub implementations.

Testing strategy (current):
Stubs enable full compile/test pass even without real implementations:
- Indexer can iterate over all six connectors, call detect(), handle
  not_found gracefully
- TUI config screen can display all six agents with "not detected"
  status
- Unit tests can mock Connector trait without needing real log files

When upgrading stub to real implementation (future commits):
1. Keep struct/impl structure identical (only replace method bodies)
2. Add private helper methods to connector impl block (e.g.,
   AmpConnector::storage_root(), ClaudeCodeConnector::parse_jsonl_session())
3. Add #[cfg(test)] mod tests with sample fixtures inline or in files
4. Update detect() to probe actual filesystem paths (not just return
   not_found)
5. Update scan() to parse real files (not just return empty Vec)

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 7.3 (Claude
Code), § 7.5 (Amp), § 7.7 (OpenCode), § 14.9 (v1 remaining connectors),
connectors/mod.rs (Connector trait), next commit (Codex/Cline/Gemini
real implementations).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
